Tension the FiberTapeÂ® suture limbs independently to remove any potential slack from under the tendon. Cut the repair sutures flush once adequate fixation is achieved. Refer back to Lateral Row Fixation to complete the repair. 5 SpeedBridgeâ„¢ Technique: Dog-Ear Fixation SpeedBridgeâ„¢ Technique: Dog-Ear Fixation I 15 Achieve anterior and posterior lateral tissue fixation using the knotless repair stitches from Knotless SwiveLockÂ® anchors. SpeedBridgeâ„¢ Technique: Dog-Ear Fixation Retrieve the knotless sutures from the lateral cannula. Load the blue repair suture onto a Scorpionâ„¢ suture passer and pass through tissue. Tension the repair suture, reducing and fixating the tendon to the bone. Cut the repair suture flush once adequate fixation is achieved. Double-Pulley With Dog-Ear Fixation Feed the end of the repair suture through the loop of the shuttle suture and fold it at the ink-mark indicator. Pull the tape shuttle suture tail to shuttle the repair suture into the knotless mechanism. NOTE: If using Knotless SwiveLock anchors and additional lateral fixation is not required, pull one end of the shuttle stitch to remove the FiberTapeÂ® sutures and cut the repair stitch using a FiberTape suture cutter. 1 3 2 16 I SpeedBridgeâ„¢ Knotless Rotator Cuff Repair SwiveLockÂ® C Suture Anchor This closed-eyelet, twist-in knotless anchor can be used as the lateral row of the SutureBridgeâ„¢ repair. Additionally, it can be combined with FiberTapeÂ® suture for SpeedFixâ„¢ and SpeedBridgeâ„¢ repairs. combines a PEEK or titanium tip with a BioComposite, PEEK, or titanium anchor body. This self-punching design can help save valuable OR time, while increasing the precision of the final construct. Combine the SwiveLock SP anchor with FiberTapeÂ® suture to complete a SpeedFixâ„¢ or SpeedBridgeâ„¢ knotless rotator cuff repair.
